# AGENTS.md

Rules every agent (including Opencode) must follow on every task in this repo.

---

## 1. Generated Files

Never modify `src/routeTree.gen.ts` directly. It is auto-generated by TanStack Router. Edit the route files under `src/routes/` instead and let the generator rebuild it.

## 2. Tenant-Scoping Changes Require Doc Updates

Never edit a `*.server.ts` file's tenant-scoping logic (any query involving `organization_id`, `tenant_id`, or row-level access control) without updating `docs/db-contracts.md` in the same change. If the file doesn't exist yet, create it.

## 3. Server Functions Need Tests

Any new `createServerFn` or exported async function in `src/lib/**/*.server.ts` that touches leads, scheduling, or notifications must include a corresponding test in `src/__tests__/`. No exceptions.

## 4. Never Weaken Tests to Pass a Build

Do not remove or weaken an existing Vitest assertion to make a build pass. Fix the code that broke the test, or flag the conflict and explain why it exists. A green test suite means the code is correct, not that the tests were silenced.

## 5. New Dependencies Require Justification

No new dependency without stating why an existing one (already in `package.json`) doesn't cover it. Name the specific gap.

## 6. Read State Docs Before Building

Before starting a task, read `docs/current-state.md` and `docs/architecture.md`. If the task assumes a capability marked **ASPIRATIONAL** in either doc, stop and say so instead of building on top of it silently.

## 7. Commit Messages Must Be Explicit

All commit messages state what changed and which doc (if any) needs a corresponding update.

## 8. Feature Freeze on Open P0s

No new subsystem (payments, encryption, RBAC, external webhooks) may be started while any P0 item from `docs/db-contracts.md` or `docs/release-readiness.md` is open.

## 9. Diagnostic Tests Must Be Marked

A test that asserts current behavior as "proof a bug exists" is a diagnostic artifact, not a fix. Any PR that documents a bug via a passing test must either:

**(a)** also fix the bug and update the test to assert correct behavior, or

**(b)** explicitly mark the test `xfail` or `skip` with a linked issue.

The test suite's pass count must never imply a known defect is resolved when it isn't.

## 10. All Commits Must Be SSH-Signed

Every commit on `main` must carry a valid SSH signature (`git config commit.gpgsign true`, `gpg.format ssh`). The signing key `~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub` is configured globally. If the agent runs `git commit` and the signature is missing, the commit is invalid — fix the config before pushing.
